phases.title: Build PhaseSummaries component (expandable per-phase summary list) with vitest coverage
verification: `apps/desktop/src/components/detail/PhaseSummaries.tsx` exports `PhaseSummaries` (component) and `deriveSummaryRows` (pure helper) using types `EditorPlan`/`EditorPhase` from `./PlanEditor`.
`deriveSummaryRows(null)` returns `[]`; `deriveSummaryRows` filters out phases whose summary is missing or whitespace-only; sorts by phase number ascending.
Component uses native `<details>` for expand/collapse — no useState toggle, no custom key handlers, no a11y workarounds.
Empty state renders when no phases have summaries.
Status chips have classes `--completed`, `--failed`, `--executing` with hex color fallbacks for the `var()` references.
`apps/desktop/src/components/__tests__/PhaseSummaries.test.tsx` adds 3 vitest cases (null plan, summary filter, sort order); all pass.
`pnpm build` clean; `pnpm test` desktop count goes from 95 to 98 (+3).
phases.summary: Created PhaseSummaries component (TSX + CSS) with exported `deriveSummaryRows` helper, reusing `EditorPlan`/`EditorPhase` from PlanEditor. Added 3 vitest cases (null plan, summary filter, sort order). pnpm build clean; desktop test count went 95 → 98.

phases.title: Mount PhaseSummaries in IssueDetail.tsx between PipelineTimeline and PlanEditor
phases.content:
Wire the new component into the existing IssueDetail layout. The plan data is already loaded; just pass it through.

### Edits to `apps/desktop/src/components/detail/IssueDetail.tsx`

1. **Import** the new component near the existing detail-component imports (around line 9):
```tsx
import { PhaseSummaries } from './PhaseSummaries';
```

2. **Mount** between the existing `<PipelineTimeline ... />` (around line 372) and `<PlanEditor ... />` (around line 443). The IssueDetail file is large — find the section that conditionally renders the plan-related UI and insert there. The exact insertion point is after the timeline closes and before the editor opens.

Pattern to insert (adapt to whatever local variable holds the loaded plan — likely `plan`):
```tsx
{plan && <PhaseSummaries plan={plan as unknown as EditorPlan} />}
```

The `as unknown as EditorPlan` cast is needed if the local `plan` state is typed `TikiPlan | null` (the type from `get_plan` IPC) rather than `EditorPlan` directly. Two options:
- **Option A (preferred)**: if `EditorPlan` and `TikiPlan` are structurally compatible (the field shapes match), import `EditorPlan` and update the local `plan` state typing.
- **Option B**: keep the cast — if there's risk of structural drift, add a TODO comment noting the types should be unified in a follow-up.

Look at the existing PlanEditor mount (line 443) to see how it casts/converts the loaded plan — mirror that exact approach. Reuse the existing transformation if there is one.

3. **Loading state**: if `loadingPlan` is true, render nothing (or a slim skeleton) — don't render the empty-state component prematurely while data is in flight. Wrap:
```tsx
{!loadingPlan && plan && <PhaseSummaries plan={plan as unknown as EditorPlan} />}
```

### Out of scope

- Don't refactor IssueDetail.tsx structure beyond the import + mount + minimal cast.
- Don't change PlanEditor or PipelineTimeline.
- Don't add new state, new IPC calls, or new selectors.
- Don't add styles to App.css — the colocated PhaseSummaries.css covers this.

### Verification — full project gates

- `pnpm build` clean (tsc -b). The cast may need adjustment if the types differ; that's the most likely friction.
- `pnpm test` — 98 desktop tests still pass (the new tests from Phase 1 are pure function; mounting the component in IssueDetail doesn't affect their pass count).
- Manual smoke (developer): open an issue with at least one completed phase having a summary; PhaseSummaries section appears with the right rows. Open a fresh issue with no plan; section either doesn't render or shows the empty state.

### Test plan

- Visual check: PhaseSummaries appears in the detail panel between the pipeline timeline and the plan editor.
- Empty case: open an issue without a plan — section either doesn't render (preferred) or shows the empty-state message.
- Populated case: open the just-shipped #110 (its plan has 3 completed phases with detailed summaries) — all 3 summaries visible, expandable.
verification: `<PhaseSummaries plan={plan} />` (or the appropriately cast equivalent) is mounted between `<PipelineTimeline>` and `<PlanEditor>` in the JSX tree.
Render is gated on `!loadingPlan && plan` so the section doesn't flash an empty state while the IPC fetch is in flight.
No edits to PlanEditor.tsx or PipelineTimeline.tsx.
`pnpm test` desktop count remains 98 (Phase 1's tests preserved); shared 65 / Rust 28 unchanged.
Manual smoke: completed-phases summaries appear in the just-shipped #110's detail panel.
phases.summary: Lifted the inline TikiPlan→EditorPlan conversion into a memoized `editorPlan` and mounted `<PhaseSummaries plan={editorPlan} />` in IssueDetail.tsx, gated on `!loadingPlan && editorPlan`. PlanEditor now consumes the same memoized value (deduplicated). pnpm build clean; desktop tests still 98.
